python - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('articles/2026/01/01/articles.jsonl')
keywords=['Bangladesh','ICC','T20','Venezuela','interim president','Starlink','Ukraine','Zelensky','economic','Syria','Aleppo','ISIL','Islamic State','AFCON','Congo','Algeria','Morocco','Cameroon','Hakimi','calf','Greenland','Denmark','Iran','Parchin','Khojir','Natanz','World Cup','immigration','ICE','Minnesota','Gaza','Qatar','Board of Peace','Arctic','wedding','Kyiv','embassy','Thailand','NIDA','aircraft carrier','Oreshnik','Grok','trade deficit','Yemen','Alimi','Sabalenka','Brisbane','Nipah','Manchester United','Ruben Amorim','Somalia','Somaliland']
kl=[k.lower() for k in keywords]
seen=set(); count=0
for line in p.open(errors='ignore'):
    try:a=json.loads(line)
    except Exception: continue
    title=a.get('title') or ''
    content=a.get('content') or ''
    txt=(title+' '+content[:800]).lower()
    hits=[keywords[i] for i,k in enumerate(kl) if k in txt]
    if hits:
        key=(title,a.get('source'),a.get('date_publish'))
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key); count+=1
        print(f"{count:03d}. {title} | {a.get('source')} | {a.get('date_publish')} | hits={','.join(hits[:5])}")
        if count>=180: break
PY

python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
m=pd.read_csv('market.csv')
print('rows',len(m),'resolved',int(m.is_resolved.sum()),'active',int((~m.is_resolved).sum()))
print('\nresolved rows:')
print(m[m.is_resolved][['qid','title','ground_truth','resolution_date','num_predictions']].sort_values('resolution_date').to_string(index=False, max_colwidth=140))
print('\nnew/near active <= 2026-01-16:')
cols=['qid','resolution_date','title','num_predictions','market_aggregate']
print(m[(~m.is_resolved)&(m.resolution_date.astype(str)<='2026-01-16')][cols].sort_values(['resolution_date','qid']).to_string(index=False, max_colwidth=140))
print('\nactive with no predictions:')
print(m[(~m.is_resolved)&(m.num_predictions==0)][['qid','resolution_date','title']].to_string(index=False, max_colwidth=120))
print('\nprediction count distribution')
print(m['num_predictions'].value_counts().sort_index().to_string())
PY
